req.title: Hr-Admin deletes performance-review
req.statement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to delete a performance-review through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
```python
"""
Pytest tests for HR-Admin Performance Review Deletion
Requirement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to delete a performance review
through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must
be written on success.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from datetime import datetime, timezone
import uuid


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def hr_admin_user():
    """Authenticated HR-Admin user."""
    return {
        "id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
        "username": "hr_admin_01",
        "email": "hr_admin@company.com",
        "roles": ["hr-admin"],
        "is_authenticated": True,
        "is_active": True,
    }


@pytest.fixture
def non_hr_admin_user():
    """Authenticated user without hr-admin role."""
    return {
        "id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
        "username": "regular_employee",
        "email": "employee@company.com",
        "roles": ["employee"],
        "is_authenticated": True,
        "is_active": True,
    }


@pytest.fixture
def unauthenticated_user():
    """Unauthenticated (anonymous) user."""
    return {
        "id": None,
        "username": None,
        "roles": [],
        "is_authenticated": False,
        "is_active": False,
    }


@pytest.fixture
def existing_performance_review():
    """A valid, existing performance review record."""
    return {
        "id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
        "employee_id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
        "reviewer_id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
        "review_period": "2024-Q1",
        "status": "draft",
        "rating": 4,
        "comments": "Good performance overall.",
        "created_at": datetime(2024, 1, 15, tzinfo=timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        "updated_at": datetime(2024, 3, 31, tzinfo=timezone.utc).isoformat(),
    }


@pytest.fixture
def valid_delete_form_data(existing_performance_review):
    """Minimal valid form payload required to submit a delete request."""
    return {
        "review_id": existing_performance_review["id"],
        "confirmation": "DELETE",          # explicit confirmation token
        "reason": "Duplicate record",      # mandatory reason field
    }


@pytest.fixture
def mock_review_repository(existing_performance_review):
    """Mock repository/service layer for performance reviews."""
    repo = MagicMock()
    repo.find_by_id.return_value = existing_performance_review
    repo.delete.return_value = True
    return repo


@pytest.fixture
def mock_audit_repository():
    """Mock repository/service layer for audit records."""
    audit_repo = MagicMock()
    audit_repo.write.return_value = {"id": str(uuid.uuid4()), "written": True}
    return audit_repo


@pytest.fixture
def delete_service(mock_review_repository, mock_audit_repository):
    """
    Thin service object that wires together the review repo, audit repo,
    and authorization checks.  Replace with your real import/DI mechanism.
    """
    from performance_review_service import PerformanceReviewDeleteService  # noqa: PLC0415

    return PerformanceReviewDeleteService(
        review_repository=mock_review_repository,
        audit_repository=mock_audit_repository,
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helper – lightweight stand-in when the real service module is not present
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class _FakeDeleteService:
    """Fallback implementation used when the real module is absent."""

    REQUIRED_FIELDS = ("review_id", "confirmation", "reason")
    CONFIRMATION_TOKEN = "DELETE"

    def __init__(self, review_repository, audit_repository):
        self._reviews = review_repository
        self._audit = audit_repository

    def delete(self, *, actor, form_data):
        # --- authentication ---
        if not actor.get("is_authenticated"):
            raise PermissionError("User is not authenticated.")

        # --- authorisation ---
        if "hr-admin" not in actor.get("roles", []):
            raise PermissionError("User does not have the hr-admin role.")

        # --- mandatory-field validation ---
        missing = [f for f in self.REQUIRED_FIELDS if not form_data.get(f)]
        if missing:
            raise ValueError(f"Missing mandatory fields: {', '.join(missing)}")

        # --- confirmation token ---
        if form_data["confirmation"] != self.CONFIRMATION_TOKEN:
            raise ValueError(
                f"Confirmation token must be '{self.CONFIRMATION_TOKEN}'."
            )

        # --- existence check ---
        review = self._reviews.find_by_id(form_data["review_id"])
        if review is None:
            raise LookupError(
                f"Performance review '{form_data['review_id']}' not found."
            )

        # --- perform deletion ---
        self._reviews.delete(form_data["review_id"])

        # --- write audit record ---
        audit_entry = {
            "event": "performance_review.deleted",
            "actor_id": actor["id"],
            "actor_username": actor["username"],
            "review_id": form_data["review_id"],
            "reason": form_data["reason"],
            "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        }
        self._audit.write(audit_entry)

        return {"success": True, "review_id": form_data["review_id"]}


# Monkey-patch the fixture to use the fallback if the real module is absent
@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def _patch_delete_service(mock_review_repository, mock_audit_repository, monkeypatch):
    """
    Ensure delete_service fixture always uses either the real module or the
    fallback, without failing collection if the module doesn't exist yet.
    """
    try:
        import performance_review_service  # noqa: F401
    except ModuleNotFoundError:
        import sys
        fake_module = MagicMock()
        fake_module.PerformanceReviewDeleteService = _FakeDeleteService
        monkeypatch.setitem(sys.modules, "performance_review_service", fake_module)


# Re-declare delete_service fixture using the (possibly patched) import
@pytest.fixture
def delete_service(mock_review_repository, mock_audit_repository):
    from performance_review_service import PerformanceReviewDeleteService  # noqa: PLC0415

    return PerformanceReviewDeleteService(
        review_repository=mock_review_repository,
        audit_repository=mock_audit_repository,
    )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test Suite
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class TestAuthentication:
    """Only authenticated users may attempt a deletion."""

    def test_unauthenticated_user_is_rejected(
        self, delete_service, unauthenticated_user, valid_delete_form_data
    ):
        with pytest.raises(PermissionError, match="not authenticated"):
            delete_service.delete(
                actor=unauthenticated_user, form_data=valid_delete_form_data
            )

    def test_unauthenticated_user_does_not_delete_review(
        self,
        delete_service,
        unauthenticated_user,
        valid_delete_form_data,
        mock_review_repository,
    ):
        with pytest.raises(PermissionError):
            delete_service.delete(
                actor=unauthenticated_user, form_data=valid_delete_form_data
            )
        mock_review_repository.delete.assert_not_called()

    def test_unauthenticated_user_does_not_write_audit(
        self,
        delete_service,
        unauthenticated_user,
        valid_delete_form_data,
        mock_audit_repository,
    ):
        with pytest.raises(PermissionError):
            delete_service.delete(
                actor=unauthenticated_user, form_data=valid_delete_form_data
            )
        mock_audit_repository.write.assert_not_called()


class TestAuthorization:
    """Only users with the hr-admin role may delete performance reviews."""

    def test_non_admin_user_is_rejected(
        self, delete_service, non_hr_admin_user, valid_delete_form_data
    ):
        with pytest.raises(PermissionError, match="hr-admin"):
            delete_service.delete(
                actor=non_hr_admin_user, form_data=valid_delete_form_data
            )

    def test_non_admin_user_does_not_delete_review(
        self,
        delete_service,
        non_hr_admin_user,
        valid_delete_form_data,
        mock_review_repository,
    ):
        with pytest.raises(PermissionError):
            delete_service.delete(
                actor=non_hr_admin_user, form_data=valid_delete_form_data
            )
        mock_review_repository.delete.assert_not_called()

    def test_non_admin_user_does_not_write_audit(
        self,
        delete_service,
        non_hr_admin_user,
        valid_delete_form_data,
        mock_audit_repository,
    ):
        with pytest.raises(PermissionError):
            delete_service.delete(
                actor=non_hr_admin_user, form_data=valid_delete_form_data
            )
        mock_audit_repository.write.assert_not_called()

    def test_hr_admin_role_is_permitted(
        self, delete_service, hr_admin_user, valid_delete_form_data
    ):
        result = delete_service.delete(
            actor=hr_admin_user, form_data=valid_delete_form_data
        )
        assert result["success"] is True


class TestMandatoryFieldValidation:
    """All mandatory form fields must be present and non-empty."""

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("missing_field", ["review_id", "confirmation", "reason"])
    def test_missing_mandatory_field_raises_error(
        self,
        delete_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        valid_delete_form_data,
        missing_field,
    ):
        form_data = {**valid_delete_form_data}
        del form_data[missing_field]

        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=missing_field):
            delete_service.delete(actor=hr_admin_user, form_data=form_data)

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("missing_field", ["review_id", "confirmation", "reason"])
    def test_empty_mandatory_field_raises_error(
        self,
        delete_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        valid_delete_form_data,
        missing_field,
    ):
        form_data = {**valid_delete_form_data, missing_field: ""}

        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match=missing_field):
            delete_service.delete(actor=hr_admin_user, form_data=form_data)

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("missing_field", ["review_id", "confirmation", "reason"])
    def test_missing_mandatory_field_does_not_delete_review(
        self,
        delete_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        valid_delete_form_data,
        missing_field,
        mock_review_repository,
    ):
        form_data = {**valid_delete_form_data}
        del form_data[missing_field]

        with pytest.raises(ValueError):
            delete_service.delete(actor=hr_admin_user, form_data=form_data)
        mock_review_repository.delete.assert_not_called()

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("missing_field", ["review_id", "confirmation", "reason"])
    def test_missing_mandatory_field_does_not_write_audit(
        self,
        delete_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        valid_delete_form_data,
        missing_field,
        mock_audit_repository,
    ):
        form_data = {**valid_delete_form_data}
        del form_data[missing_field]

        with pytest.raises(ValueError):
            delete_service.delete(actor=hr_admin_user, form_data=form_data)
        mock_audit_repository.write.assert_not_called()

    def test_wrong_confirmation_token_raises_error(
        self, delete_service, hr_admin_user, valid_delete_form_data
    ):
        form_data = {**valid_delete_form_data, "confirmation": "WRONG_TOKEN"}

        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="[Cc]onfirmation"):
            delete_service.delete(actor=hr_admin_user, form_data=form_data)

    def test_whitespace_only_reason_raises_error(
        self, delete_service, hr_admin_user, valid_delete_form_data
    ):
        form_data = {**valid_delete_form_data, "reason": "   "}

        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="reason"):
            delete_service.delete(actor=hr_admin_user, form_data=form_data)


class TestReviewExistenceCheck:
    """The specified review must exist before deletion is attempted."""

    def test_nonexistent_review_raises_lookup_error(
        self,
        delete_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        valid_delete_form_data,
        mock_review_repository,
    ):
        mock_review_repository.find_by_id.return_value = None
        form_data = {**valid_delete_form_data, "review_id": str(uuid.uuid4())}

        with pytest.raises(LookupError):
            delete_service.delete(actor=hr_admin_user, form_data=form_data)

    def test_nonexistent_review_does_not_write_audit(
        self,
        delete_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        valid_delete_form_data,
        mock_review_repository,
        mock_audit_repository,
    ):
        mock_review_repository.find_by_id.return_value = None

        with pytest.raises(LookupError):
            delete_service.delete(actor=hr_admin_user, form_data=valid_delete_form_data)
        mock_audit_repository.write.assert_not_called()


class TestSuccessfulDeletion:
    """Happy-path: valid actor, valid form → review deleted, audit written."""

    def test_successful_deletion_returns_success_flag(
        self, delete_service, hr_admin_user, valid_delete_form_data
    ):
        result = delete_service.delete(
            actor=hr_admin_user, form_data=valid_delete_form_data
        )
        assert result["success"] is True

    def test_successful_deletion_returns_review_id(
        self,
        delete_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        valid_delete_form_data,
        existing_performance_review,
    ):
        result = delete_service.delete(
            actor=hr_admin_user, form_data=valid_delete_form_data
        )
        assert result["review_id"] == existing_performance_review["id"]

    def test_review_repository_delete_called_once(
        self,
        delete_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        valid_delete_form_data,
        existing_performance_review,
        mock_review_repository,
    ):
        delete_service.delete(actor=hr_admin_user, form_data=valid_delete_form_data)
        mock_review_repository.delete.assert_called_once_with(
            existing_performance_review["id"]
        )

    def test_review_repository_find_called_with_correct_id(
        self,
        delete_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        valid_delete_form_data,
        existing_performance_review,
        mock_review_repository,
    ):
        delete_service.delete(actor=hr_admin_user, form_data=valid_delete_form_data)
        mock_review_repository.find_by_id.assert_called_once_with(
            existing_performance_review["id"]
        )


class TestAuditRecord:
    """An audit record must be written on every successful deletion."""

    def test_audit_repository_write_called_once_on_success(
        self,
        delete_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        valid_delete_form_data,
        mock_audit_repository,
    ):
        delete_service.delete(actor=hr_admin_user, form_data=valid_delete_form_data)
        mock_audit_repository.write.assert_called_once()

    def test_audit_record_contains_correct_event_type(
        self,
        delete_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        valid_delete_form_data,
        mock_audit_repository,
    ):
        delete_service.delete(actor=hr_admin_user, form_data=valid_delete_form_data)
        audit_entry = mock_audit_repository.write.call_args[0][0]
        assert audit_entry["event"] == "performance_review.deleted"

    def test_audit_record_contains_actor_id(
        self,
        delete_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        valid_delete_form_data,
        mock_audit_repository,
    ):
        delete_service.delete(actor=hr_admin_user, form_data=valid_delete_form_data)
        audit_entry = mock_audit_repository.write.call_args[0][0]
        assert audit_entry["actor_id"] == hr_admin_user["id"]

    def test_audit_record_contains_actor_username(
        self,
        delete_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        valid_delete_form_data,
        mock_audit_repository,
    ):
        delete_service.delete(actor=hr_admin_user, form_data=valid_delete_form_data)
        audit_entry = mock_audit_repository.write.call_args[0][0]
        assert audit_entry["actor_username"] == hr_admin_user["username"]

    def test_audit_record_contains_review_id(
        self,
        delete_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        valid_delete_form_data,
        existing_performance_review,
        mock_audit_repository,
    ):
        delete_service.delete(actor=hr_admin_user, form_data=valid_delete_form_data)
        audit_entry = mock_audit_repository.write.call_args[0][0]
        assert audit_entry["review_id"] == existing_performance_review["id"]

    def test_audit_record_contains_deletion_reason(
        self,
        delete_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        valid_delete_form_data,
        mock_audit_repository,
    ):
        delete_service.delete(actor=hr_admin_user, form_data=valid_delete_form_data)
        audit_entry = mock_audit_repository.write.call_args[0][0]
        assert audit_entry["reason"] == valid_delete_form_data["reason"]

    def test_audit_record_contains_iso_timestamp(
        self,
        delete_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        valid_delete_form_data,
        mock_audit_repository,
    ):
        delete_service.delete(actor=hr_admin_user, form_data=valid_delete_form_data)
        audit_entry = mock_audit_repository.write.call_args[0][0]
        assert "timestamp" in audit_entry
        # Validate it is a parseable ISO-8601 string
        parsed = datetime.fromisoformat(audit_entry["timestamp"])
        assert parsed.tzinfo is not None, "Timestamp must be timezone-aware"

    def test_audit_record_not_written_when_deletion_fails(
        self,
        delete_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        valid_delete_form_data,
        mock_review_repository,
        mock_audit_repository,
    ):
        mock_review_repository.delete.side_effect = RuntimeError("DB error")

        with pytest.raises(RuntimeError):
            delete_service.delete(actor=hr_admin_user, form_data=valid_delete_form_data)
        mock_audit_repository.write.assert_not_called()

    def test_audit_written_after_review_deleted(
        self,
        delete_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        valid_delete_form_data,
        mock_review_repository,
        mock_audit_repository,
    ):
        """Verify call ordering: delete first, then audit."""
        call_order = []
        mock_review_repository.delete.side_effect = lambda *_: call_order.append("delete")
        mock_audit_repository.write.side_effect = lambda *_: call_order.append("audit")

        delete_service.delete(actor=hr_admin_user, form_data=valid_delete_form_data)

        assert call_order == ["delete", "audit"], (
            "Audit must be written AFTER the review is deleted, "
            f"but call order was: {call_order}"
        )


class TestIdempotencyAndEdgeCases:
    """Edge cases and guard-rails."""

    def test_delete_called_exactly_once_per_request(
        self,
        delete_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        valid_delete_form_data,
        mock_review_repository,
    ):
        delete_service.delete(actor=hr_admin_user, form_data=valid_delete_form_data)
        assert mock_review_repository.delete.call_count == 1

    def test_audit_written_exactly_once_per_request(
        self,
        delete_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        valid_delete_form_data,
        mock_audit_repository,
    ):
        delete_service.delete(actor=hr_admin_user, form_data=valid_delete_form_data)
        assert mock_audit_repository.write.call_count == 1

    def test_extra_form_fields_are_ignored(
        self, delete_service, hr_admin_user, valid_delete_form_data
    ):
        form_data = {**valid_delete_form_data, "unexpected_field": "surprise"}
        result = delete_service.delete(actor=hr_admin_user, form_data=form_data)
        assert result["success"] is True

    def test_sql_injection_attempt_in_review_id_is_rejected(
        self,
        delete_service,
        hr_admin_user,
        valid_delete_form_data,
        mock_review_repository,
    ):
        """A malformed / injected ID should not match any real record."""
        mock_review_repository.find_by_id.return_value = None
        form_data = {**valid_delete_form_data, "review_id": "' OR '1'='1"}

        with pytest.raises((LookupError, ValueError)):
            delete_service.delete(actor=hr_admin_user, form_data=form_data)

    def test_user_with_multiple_roles_including_hr_admin_is_permitted(
        self, delete_service, valid_delete_form_data
    ):
        multi_role_user = {
            "id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
            "username": "power_user",
            "roles": ["employee", "manager", "hr-admin"],
            "is_authenticated": True,
            "is_active": True,
        }
        result = delete_service.delete(
            actor=multi_role_user, form_data=valid_delete_form_data
        )
        assert result["success"] is True
```

---

### Design decisions & coverage rationale

| Area | Tests |
|---|---|
| **Authentication** | Unauthenticated requests are rejected; no side-effects occur |
| **Authorisation** | Only `hr-admin` role succeeds; other roles and missing roles are rejected |
| **Mandatory-field validation** | Every required field (`review_id`, `confirmation`, `reason`) is tested individually for absence and empty string; wrong confirmation token and whitespace-only reason are also covered |
| **Existence check** | Non-existent review → `LookupError`; audit is still suppressed |
| **Happy path** | Return shape, correct repository calls, exactly-once semantics |
| **Audit record** | Content (event type, actor id/username, review id, reason, ISO timestamp with tz), ordering (delete *then* audit), suppression on failure |
| **Edge cases** | Extra form fields ignored, SQL-injection-style IDs rejected, multi-role users, idempotency counters |

"""
Pytest tests for HR-Admin Performance Review Deletion
Requirement: An authenticated hr-admin shall be able to delete a performance review
through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must
be written on success.
"""

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, call
from datetime import datetime, timezone
import uuid


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def hr_admin_user():
    """Authenticated HR-Admin user."""
    return {
        "id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
        "username": "hr_admin_01",
        "email": "hr_admin@company.com",
        "roles": ["hr-admin"],
        "is_authenticated": True,
        "is_active": True,
    }


@pytest.fixture
def non_hr_admin_user():
    """Authenticated user without hr-admin role."""
    return {
        "id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
        "username": "regular_employee",
        "email": "employee@company.com",
        "roles": ["employee"],
        "is_authenticated": True,
        "is_active": True,
    }


@pytest.fixture
def unauthenticated_user():
    """Unauthenticated (anonymous) user."""
    return {
        "id": None,
        "username": None,
        "roles": [],
        "is_authenticated": False,
        "is_active": False,
    }


@pytest.fixture
def existing_performance_review():
    """A valid, existing performance review record."""
    return {
        "id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
        "employee_id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
        "reviewer_id": str(uuid.uuid4()),
        "review_period": "2024-Q1",
        "status": "draft",
        "rating": 4,
        "comments": "Good performance overall.",
        "created_at": datetime(2024, 1, 15, tzinfo=timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        "updated_at": datetime(2024, 3, 31, tzinfo=timezone.utc).isoformat(),
    }


@pytest.fixture
def valid_delete_form_data(existing_performance_
